table_semantics.justification: Full table structure used: <table>, <caption>, <thead>, <tbody>, and all header cells use <th scope='col'>. Caption clearly describes the table's purpose. Not used for layout.

element_choice.justification: All interactive elements are native: <button> for Download CSV, <input type='search'> for search, <select> for filters, <a> elements for pagination. Dates use <time> with datetime attributes. No div/span used where semantic elements exist.

form_semantics.justification: Every input and select has a visible <label> element. Controls are grouped in a <search> wrapping a <form>. Native select elements used for dropdowns. No placeholder-as-label pattern.

aria_discipline.justification: ARIA used sparingly and only where needed: aria-label on <search> and <nav>, aria-current='page' on the current pagination link, aria-hidden on decorative SVG and span characters. No redundant ARIA roles on native elements.

heading_hierarchy.justification:
Single

in
for 'Employee Directory' is correct. However, the
contains a

for 'Acme Corp' rather than a heading — acceptable since the page h1 is the primary heading. The visually-hidden h2 for the section is appropriate. Minor: two h1-like elements could be flagged since header has no heading at all, but this is not a hierarchy violation.

landmark_structure.justification: Skip links present for both main content and search. <header>, <main>, <search>, <section aria-labelledby>, and <nav aria-label> all used correctly. <main> contains only primary page content. All navigations and the search landmark are labelled.

content_realism.justification: Eight rows of realistic, diverse employee data with varied names, departments, roles, dates, and statuses. No placeholder content. 'Showing 8 of 42 employees' reflects plausible real-world pagination context.

list_semantics.justification: Pagination items are wrapped in <ol> within <nav>, which is appropriate as page order matters. No other list contexts are applicable to this page structure.

expectations_met: Status column content is not conveyed by colour alone — text labels ('Active', 'On Leave', 'Departed') are present inside the badge
Department and status filters use native <select> elements
Filter controls are grouped in a <search> element wrapping a <form>
Pagination links clearly indicate current page with aria-current='page'
Previous/Next are <a> elements with appropriate rel attributes
Table is not wrapped in role='grid'
No CSS display properties strip table semantics
additional_observations: Excellent use of <search> element wrapping the <form> for the filter toolbar
Skip links provided for both main content and the search/filter area — thorough keyboard accessibility
Dates use <time datetime='...'> with machine-readable ISO format and human-readable display text
SVG icon in Download CSV button has aria-hidden='true' and focusable='false' — correct pattern

The
contains only a

for 'Acme Corp' with no heading — minor, but acceptable since the page h1 serves as the primary title
